title: “Manager accused of selling drugs at McDonald’s”
Pesto Puff Pastry Twists
description: Fast food is a type of mass-produced food designed for commercial resale and with a strong priority placed on "speed of service" versus other relevant factors involved in culinary science. Fast food was originally created as a commercial strategy to accommodate the larger numbers of busy commuters, travelers and wage workers who often did not have the time to sit down at a public house or diner and wait for their meal. By making speed of service the priority, this ensured that customers with strictly limited time (a commuter stopping to procure dinner to bring home to their family, for example, or an hourly laborer on a short lunch break) were not inconvenienced by waiting for their food to be cooked on-the-spot (as is expected from a traditional "sit down" restaurant). For those with no time to spare, fast food became a multibillion-dollar industry. Describing food is not as easy as it would seem. How many ways can you say something was really tasty? You can compare a dish to a picture, a sports game, a musical performance or give it personality. Describe its sunny or somber mood, shyness, assertiveness, or contradiction of flavors. Knowing the words for describing tastes and flavors will help you to find the right adjective in other areas.

title: ISM 2019: A New RUBY Era | Barry Callebaut
description: With the unveiling of Ruby as the fourth type of chocolate we launched one of the greatest breakthroughs in chocolate in decades in Shanghai in 2017. Since its launch, we have seen many consumer and artisanal brands, including Prestat, Baci Perugina, KitKat, Leonidas and Fazer, introducing Ruby in their own products for their consumers in Asia Pacific, Europe, the Middle East and South Africa. At ISM 2019, we painted the town RUBY and brought chefs from around the world to come together, with our customers and the press, to offer workshops and showcase Ruby’s signature creations in confectionery, drinks, bakery, dairy and desserts. Eleven of our customers are introducing their own Ruby chocolate products, and that’s only at this year’s ISM. At ISM 2019, we clearly gave our customers an even bigger reason to visit the ‘BC Studio’ dedicated to Ruby, to taste and experience the intense sensorial delight offered by Ruby. As we continue to build Ruby chocolate as the fourth category of chocolate, we look forward to many more Ruby launches over the next coming months and years. Ruby chocolate is THE food trend to watch. From Saturday Night Live hailing its U.S. launch to making ranks for being the most innovative products in lists by the American (NY Mag, National Confectioners Association), Australian (Smooth FM), Danish (7-Eleven), Indian (Harper’s Bazaar India), Japanese (Nikkei trendy), Middle Eastern (Gulfood Manufacturing Industry Excellence Awards 2018) and even global associations and media (Foodbev), Ruby chocolate has clearly seen its global renown skyrocket since its launch. Ruby even made it on the dessert menu at the Official Afterparty of the Oscars, offering the guests and celebrities of Hollywood the taste of the berry-fruitiness and luscious smoothness of the fourth category of chocolate. #BarryCallebaut #ISM2019 #RubyChocolate ► Read more on: https://rubychocolate.com --- More information: https://www.barry-callebaut.com/ Follow us: Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/BarryCallebautGroup/ LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/barry-callebaut-group/

title: German-Style Braised Red Cabbage | Recipes | Whole Foods Market 365
description: German-style Braised Red Cabbage Makes 8 to 10 servings Cabbage has a bad reputation for being bland and stinky, but this sweet and sour version is anything but boring. Serve it alongside classic German dishes, like sausages or schnitzel, or with simple grilled chicken or roasted pork. 1 cup cider vinegar 2 tablespoons light or dark brown sugar 2 tablespoon raspberry jelly 1 teaspoon kosher salt ½ teaspoon ground black pepper 2 strips thick-sliced bacon, cut into ½-inch slices 1 head red cabbage, cored and thinly sliced 1 apple, such as Fiji or Gala, peeled and thinly sliced 1. In a small bowl, combine vinegar, sugar, jelly, salt and pepper, and set aside. 2. Heat a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-low heat. Add bacon and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned all over and crisp, about 6 minutes. Drain all but about 1 tablespoon of bacon fat. 3. Add cabbage, apple, and reserved vinegar mixture. Stir to coat. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until the cabbage is deep purple and tender, about 50 minutes. Share your creations with us with #GoodThings365 About Whole Foods Market 365: Whole Foods Market 365™ is changing the grocery shopping experience. Committed to making healthy living easy, 365 provides simple, affordable and convenient everyday shopping to neighborhoods nationwide without sacrificing the quality standards and dedication to food transparency that Whole Foods Market pioneered. Whole Foods Market 365 features a thoughtful product selection curated for convenience, including the 365 Everyday Value® brand customers know and love, alongside other high-quality brands and new offerings from innovative supplier partners.

"Amazing atmosphere and incredible vegan food and plenty of options to choose from. Such a welcoming vibe from decor to location, overall hot spot to grab a bit or chill. Love, love, love this place!" Plant is a vegan community event center consisting of a restaurant, concession stand, kava lounge, art gallery & theatre. ------------------------------------ Original music by Werner Elmker using the 'Native Instruments' sample of the Una Corda. This instrument was originally created by David Klavins for German pianist and composer Nils Frahm It is an exquisite piano which uses only one string per key ('una corda' is Italian for 'one string') instead of the usual two or three, and has no enclosure, revealing its asymmetrical stainless steel frame. It is sampled by Native Instruments in intimate detail to deliver everything from up-close realism to resonant overtones. Please check this video for more information about this unique instrument: https://youtu.be/gfHK7_lSY-0 Werner Elmker is an independent Danish/American video producer, photographer, graphic designer, concert pianist and composer. His video work and photos capturing life in Fairfield, Iowa has attracted numerous individuals and families to move there. In videography, photography and music, Werner Elmker emphasizes the importance of being in tune with the flow of the moment. title: Globally unique green energy facility in Denmark
description: In spring 2018, the vision to set new standards for cost-competitive renewable heat and power production became a reality with the official opening of a revolutionary green energy facility in Brønderslev, in the Northern part of Denmark. The system is the first combined heat and power (CHP) plant in the world to integrate concentrated solar power (CSP) and a biomass boiler while also using Organic Ranking Cycle (ORC to turn the energy into district heating and electricity. title: Den første pølse på Mars // Plantepølsen på COOP Crowdfunding
description: Link til kampagnen: https://crowdfunding.coop.dk/project/408 Kødfri dag? Smag vores lækre og næringsrige plantepølser og plantefars lavet af økologisk overskudsgrønt - som NASA ville gøre det på Mars Pølsen udvikledes i sin tid på baggrund af fødevaremangel og ressourcespild. Mon ikke det er på tide at vende tilbage til pølsen, nu hvor effektivitet og kreativitet ift. vores mad atter er på dagsordenen? Kødproduktion er en kæmpe belastning for miljøet og hvis flere danskere skal spise flere grøntsager og mindre kød, så er det afgørende, at kvaliteten og smagen af de grønne produkter er spændende og overbevisende. Det er her Plantepølsen kommer ind i billedet. EN BID AF FREMTIDEN Plantepølsen laver lokal-producerede kvalitetsprodukter - naturlige, næringsrige og velsmagende og med en høj procentdel rene grøntsager - som supplement til en sund livsstil. Ambitionen er at få bredt budskabet ud til supermarkeder og mange flere steder. I dag udvikler, producerer og sælger vi plantebaserede kvalitetspølser fra en lille butik i Jægersborggade på Nørrebro. Vores egen unikke plantefars fungerer både som fyld i pølsen og som fars til deller og bøffer. Vores mål er at skabe en velsmagende fars og en plantebaseret pølse med den rette konsistens og et sprødt knæk. I samarbejde med DTU udvikler vi lige nu på selve pølseskindet og vi søger her penge til at sætte vores første større fars-produktion igang. Vi håber du vil støtte vores kampagne og derved hjælpe os et skridt videre mod målet om at blive DEN FØRSTE PØLSE PÅ MARS! OVERSKUDSGRØNT Farsen består af hele 60% grøntsager - som det eneste produkt på markedet. Grøntsagerne er ren pulp, som genanvendes fra økologisk, koldpresset juice, hvilket gør pølsen til et vigtigt led i en cirkulær økonomi. I Danmark produceres omkring 100.000 ton økologiske grøntsager om året, hvoraf op mod 30 % kasseres pga. forkert størrelse eller form. Altså grøntsager, der lige nu overvejende bliver kørt til biogasanlæg, og som kunne bruges til mad i stedet. Vores ambition er at udnytte sæsonens grønt inden det kasseres, og derved være med til at optimere danskernes resourceforbrug. IDEEN Det hele startede med en fælles drøm om at gøre en forskel og et ønske om at være med til at præge den verden, vi er en del af, i en mere bæredygtig retning. Ideen til Plantepølsen udsprang af spørgsmålet “hvordan introducerer vi god plantebaseret mad til en bredere skare?”. Mange tror, at plantebaseret mad er kedeligt, besværligt, og at det ikke kan give dem nok proteiner. Den opfattelse ville vi gerne lave om på - og med hotdog’en som en slags trojansk hest kastede vi os over udfordringen. Vi tror på, at den plantebaserede bølge ikke blot er en tendens, men er noget, der er kommet for at blive, og vores ambition er at være med til at forankre denne bølge i nye grønne og velsmagende traditioner - til gavn for planeten og menneskeheden. VORES REJSE 7. April 2017: Plantepølsens første event i Roskilde 15. Marts 2018: Butikslokalet i Jægersborggade 39 overtages 27. April 2018: Succesfuld Kickstarter crowdfunding kampagne (se link nedenfor) 4. Juli 2018: Butikken i Jægersborggade åbner 9. September 2018: Deltagelse ved Hotdog DM i Aarhus 12. December 2018: InnoBooster-bevilling (fondsmidler) er i hus BELØNNINGER Vi har lavet forskellige belønninger i håb om at der vil være noget for enhver. Til den lækkersultne har vi lavet en belønning med en af vores egne hotdog, som kan indløses i vores butik i Jægersborggade umiddelbart efter kampagnens afslutning. Til hjemmekokken har vi lavet belønninger som gør det muligt at prøve farsen i madlavningen derhjemme. farsen vil ligeledes kunne hentes i butikken. Til innovatøren findes der en belønning med fremtidens plantepølser med knæk - lige til at lægge på grillen. Lykkes det os at lave det rigtige skind, vil du være blandt de første til at få smag for vores unikke produkt. Til den sociale har vi lavet en belønningspakke med mad til en grill fest, hvor produkterne kan testes af med venner og bekendte i et afslappet miljø. FØLG MED I UDVIKLINGEN Find os på nettet og de sociale medier. www.plantepolsen.dk www.instagram.com/plantepolsen www.facebook.com/plantepolsen Ønsker du at læse og høre mere om vores historie er her et par links, hvor der bliver gået mere i dybden. https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/0liverlewis/planteplsens-vegan-hotdog-diner-in-copenhagen http://plantetinget.dk/?p=486 https://medium.com/@0liverlewis/rend-mig-i-traditionerne-219542487f5a Mange tak for din tid og din støtte! title: Sustainable Energy Video | Orbak Biomass
description: Sustainable Energy Video | Orbak Biomass A quick introduction to Denmark's green transition Denmark is on the road to a greener future, but we are not quite there yet. Let us share experiences and inspire each other to continue the journey. Watch a quick animated summary of where we are heading. The movie is funded by the Confederation of Danish Industry and developed in cooperation with the State of Green Biogas is a type of biofuel that arises from the breakdown of organic waste. Produced in anaerobic environments (those devoid of oxygen), biogas is a low-cost and reliable fuel source that lowers greenhouse gas emissions, reduces landfill, thereby protecting the water supply from toxic elements. Denmark is working towards eliminating the use of natural gas and is the leading country in Europe based on the share of biogas in the gas transmission system. ➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖ We contribute to sustainable energy​ Bioenergy is the energy stored in organic matter or biomass. The energy comes from sunlight that plants exploit as they grow. The biomass can be burned directly or processed into various types of fuels, e.g. wood pellets, biogas or bioethanol Year after year, Danish companies are better at sorting food waste because they think it into their environmental policy and in this way, many tons of waste are released to produce biogas. The food waste that might previously end up on the incineration ends today at Orbak Biomass, where we carefully sort and pre-treat the food waste and convert it into high-quality biomass for the benefit of the Danish biogas companies. The organic material is extracted by biogas plants that extract biogas from the product and in this way ensures all energy in the form of heat, electricity, and fuel and at the same time leaves a product that farmers can use as fertilizer for their fields where they can grow crops. which we subsequently use in our cooking and eating … and so it all becomes a part of a large recycle chain. We call this sustainable energy … and Orbak Biomass will be a part of that. We think we are extremely privileged to make a difference every day and help make the world a better place to live. So please contact us if you think we can help you too. ➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖ Contact with Us ⬇️⬇️⬇️⬇️⬇️ ✅Telephone : +45 4440 4440 ✅email: info@orbakbiomass.com ✅website: http://www.orbakbiomass.com/

title: [013] Copenhagen in Denmark
channel: Michelle Purse - My Outsourced Marketing Team
description: Source: https://www.podbean.com/media/share/pb-9vewt-9cb715 Explore Europe Copenhagen in Denmark Podcast Episode Show Notes Home to the children’s story teller, Hans Christian Andersson, the city of Copenhagen is the capital of Denmark and very much the beating heart of the country. It’s a very advanced city in terms of infrastructure, architecture, sustainability, tourism and culture. Which makes it such a joy to visit and get around. Copenhagen is one of my favourite cities in Europe and as I grew up reading Hans Christian Andersen Fairytales when I went there I had to make a pilgrimage to the little mermaid of course! Here are the links from our Explore Europe Copenhagen episode: Harbour Cruise: https://www.stromma.dk/kobenhavn/sightseeing/sightseeing-med-bad/grand-tour/ Vor Frelsers Kirke/Church of Our Saviour: http://www.vorfrelserskirke.dk Christiansborg (another tower): http://kongeligeslotte.dk/en/palaces-and-gardens/christiansborg-palace.html Copenhagen Card: https://copenhagencard.com Tivoli Gardens: www.tivoli.dk Cafes & Restaurants Grospiseri restaraunt on top of the old car auction building! (book in advance): https://www.grospiseri.dk Manfreds restaurant: http://manfreds.dk/en/ Relæ http://www.restaurant-relae.dk/en/ Noma the best restaurant in the world (very experience, book well in advance): http://noma.dk Reffen (street food): https://reffen.dk Torvehallerne food hall: https://torvehallernekbh.dk Souls plant based restaurant: http://www.soulscph.dk Mad og Kaffe coffee hause: https://madogkaffe.dk Accommodation: Budget hostels Generator: https://generatorhostels.com/destinations/copenhagen Dan Hostel: https://www.danhostel.dk/en Budget Hotels Cabinn: https://www.cabinn.com/hoteller-koebenhavn-copenhagen Wake Up: https://www.wakeupcopenhagen.dk/ Neighbourhood guides: Nyhavn: https://www.visitcopenhagen.com/copenhagen/nyhavn-gdk474735 Vesterbro: https://www.visitcopenhagen.com/copenhagen/hipster-vesterbro Frederiksberg: https://www.visitcopenhagen.com/copenhagen/posh-frederiksberg-0 Nørrebro: https://www.visitcopenhagen.com/copenhagen/multicultural-norrebro-0 Meatpacking District: https://www.visitcopenhagen.com/copenhagen/meatpacking-district Discover more of Jen Gale's easy to implement sustainable living tips here: www.asustainablelife.co.uk Melanie’s Links: Dejlige Days Blog: www.dejligedays.com YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCKBGJkKT0SG-t-uKglBHMOQ Dejlige Days Welcome: www.dejligedayswelcome.com Podcast Conversation Timeline: 00:01- Welcome to Explore Europe and a big welcome to the show, Melanie Haynes! Where are we exploring today? 00:47 - How did Melanie end up living in Copenhagen? 2:37 - How difficult is it to adjust to the dark winters of Denmark? 3:16 - Melanie explains what Hygge is and why is it an important part of Danish life 6:27 - What shall we do when we visit Copenhagen, Melanie? 9:24 - Although we’re missing John in this week’s episode, Melanie steps in to give us the nooks and crannies of the area - thanks, Mel! 9:50 - And…..even the hop on, hop off bus gets a mention! 10:18 - Information about the Copenhagen card and taking the boat tours 12:14 - A must see: Tivoli Gardens - What to do there - What fare is on offer - What ticket should you buy 18:31 - Michelle digs deeper into what else Melanie would suggest to see in Copenhagen? 18: 39 - Canal cruise and Nyhavn 18:56 - Melanie tells us all about the Smørrebrød (the traditional open sandwich). Plus, Melanie teaches Michelle how to pronounce it. 20:10 - The longest walking street in Europe 20:31 - The neighbourhoods of Copenhagen 22:07 - Melanie and Michelle talks about ‘Danish Style’ 22:37 - The cafe culture 24:20 - It’s food time. Melanie gives us her favourite spots to eat and tips for eating out on a budget 28:05 - The street food of Copenhagen 29:41 - Isn’t it all pork based dishes in Denmark? Melanie dispels the myths and offers some tips for all the vegetarians listening 31:15 - Where is the best (and worst) places to stay in the city? 34:34 - How do we get to Copenhagen? Where is the airport and what transport is best to get around? 36:23 - What airlines fly in and out of Copenhagen? 38:15 - What is the currency and can I use my credit card? 39:55 - Do the locals speak English in Denmark? 41:10 - Getting in touch with Melanie and how she can help you. 43:31 - Michelle’s overview of Copenhagen 44:09 - Sustainable Travel Tip by Jen Gayle A Massive Thank You For Listening! Hearing these insider tips from Melanie makes me want to go back again so if you do visit, please write in and tell us all about your trip. We would love to hear all about your trip and any tips you have for fellow explorers. Leave a comment below or tweet us at @explore_europe and use the hashtag #ExploreEurope Please leave a comment/subscribe/tell a friend

title: Lundsby Biogas - Madsen Bioenergi - ENGLISH SUBTITLES
description: In the autumn of 2014, Lundsby commissioned the largest biogas plant so far at Madsen Bioenergi. Madsen Bioenergi was built near Balling northwest of Skive, Denmark. The plant was designed to process a minimum of 130,000 tons of biomass a year, but can easily process much more. The production had been designed for about 3.3 million m3 of natural biomethane – at the moment, however, almost 5 million m3 of natural biomethane are processed per year. The natural biomethane is distributed by HMN Naturgas and distributed via the common natural gas grid. Follow us on our social media: Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/lundsbybiogas.dk) Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/lundsby_biogas/) LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/company/lundsby-biogas-a-s/)

title: WICE4Soil - Waste Insects and Circular Economy for soil improvement
description: The conventional management practices of catering waste and former foods are currently down-cycling the resources by turning waste (bio-pulp) into energy – heat, electricity, and biogas – whilst the digest from biogas production is dispersed as agricultural soil improvement. In this talk, insect specialist Anton Gligorescu from The Danish Technological Institute (DTI) shares insights on how to create a new symbiosis between the insect and biogas sector, which could lead to both more sustainable food waste management and to new business possibilities. Learn more: https://www.dti.dk/wice4soil-and-8211-waste-insects-and-circular-economy-for-soil-improvement/42384

title: Relæ – Christian Puglisi Closes His Restaraunt After 10 Years
description: Relæ is a New Nordic restaurant from Italian chef and Noma alumni Christian Puglisi. Head chef Jonathan Tam has a unique style of cooking and follows Puglisi's philosophy of being as close as possible to 100% organic and sustainable. While not an easy task, Puglisi and his team have made great strides with initiatives like their Farm of Ideas as well as their Seed Exchange program, which have both involved the global food community and helped spread knowledge about these topics to the industry. These efforts awarded them a top score in the 360 Eat Guide in 2019. After 10 years, Relæ will be closing at the end of 2020. title: Transform af 1994 ApS´ Biofertilizer production test project at Danvan A/S - www.rootzone.dk
description: Waste to Value *Transform af 1994 ApS ´s high temperature composting technology can transfer waste to Bio-fertilizers in 5-10 days. *The bio-fertilizers produced can suppress the incidence of pests and plant diseases. *Helps in reducing input expenses by replacing the cost of chemical fertilizers. *The biofertilizers produced improve the soils structure by influencing the aggregation of the soil particles and help in better water retention. *Biofertilizers also enhance the availability of plant nutrients which helps in maintenance of the soil fertility over a long period of time. *The biofertilizers stimulate plant growth through the synthesis of growth-promoting substances.

title: Kathrin Rousk | Cyanobacterial nitrogen fixation in pristine ecosystems
description: Kathrin Rousk - University of Copenhagen, Denmark Homepage: https://www1.bio.ku.dk/english/research/te/ecosystem-ecology/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/kathrin_rousk Cyanobacterial nitrogen fixation in pristine ecosystems: Uncovering the climatic, ecological, and molecular control mechanisms Nitrogen (N2) fixation performed by moss-associated cyanobacteria is one of the main sources of new N input in pristine ecosystems like boreal forests and arctic tundra. Here, moss-cyanobacteria associations can contribute more than 50% to total ecosystem N input, thereby sustaining plant productivity in these pristine systems. However, N2 fixation in mosses is strongly influenced by abiotic factors, such as moisture and temperature. Here, I will present results from field and laboratory assessments on the controls of moss-associated N2 fixation, and I will give insights into our current research on moss host specificity and cyanobacterial community composition on mosses across climates. Finally, I will give a brief outlook on our future plans on identifying the nature of the relationship mosses and associated cyanobacteria share. This talk is part of the CyanoWorld online seminar series, hosted by Nicolas Schmelling and Ilka Axmann, and was held on Zoom on December 10th, 2020. Follow Us on Twitter: https://twitter.com/CyanoWorld1

title: NGRF project – 1st technical tour at I/S Reno-Nord (Aalborg, DK) – Nov 13, 2018
description: This video is about the technical tour of 13th November 2018 that the NextGenRoadFuels (NGRF) consortium did at I/S Reno-Nord, a waste management company based in Aalborg (DK). In NGRF, we care of high-efficient techniques for sorting urban residues in European cities. For the scope of the project, we will focus on biogenic waste and sewage sludge that need to be duly collected and sorted, in order to be treated and becoming high-quality feedstock for the Hydrothermal Liquefaction process. NGRF project will develop a competitive European technology platform for sustainable liquid fuel production technologies, proving the Hydrothermal Liquefaction (HTL) pathway as an efficient route to produce high-volume, cost-competitive drop-in synthetic gasoline and diesel fuels as well as other hydrocarbon compounds. This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 818413. For all the information, drop an email to: info@nextgenroadfuels.eu

title: Burning Boat Show | EP 62 “Souls"
description: https://www.patreon.com/burningboat We are so excited to finally have our friends from Souls on the podcast! Souls just won Vegan Restaurant of the year here in Copenhagen and they were actually one of our first clients back in 2018. We talk about everything from starting a business with your own money to why being vegan is something anyone can do to help improve the environment and live a more compassionate lifestyle. Here are some words from Soul's website to give you a better idea of what they are all about... "At Souls, we promise to deliver healthy, locally sourced food of the highest quality for people that are conscious not only of what they put into their body, but the footprint it leaves on the planet in order to reach their plate. Whether it’s breakfast, brunch, lunch or dinner, we are here for ya! Founded by two Australians, Jason and Matt and two kitchen gurus with years of international and local cuisine experience, Souls keeps the Australian approach of being straight forward yet laid back. We are honest about our produce and want to offer a plant based alternative for people who want worry-free food on the go with the highest quality. Copenhagen has voted for us as the City’s Best Vegan and Most Sustainable Restaurant for takeaway in 2018, and Best Vegan Restaurant by the Vegan Society in Denmark 2020. We recognize that any of our successes are because of our bad ass guests and are super stoked when you visit us and have (and always will) love to hear what you thought of your experience. So don’t be shy, come on down(under) and lets change the world together! " You can check them out at https://www.soulscph.dk/ and https://www.instagram.com/soulscph/ Check out our Website - https://www.whyburningboat.com/ Follow us on Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/WhyBurningBoat/ Follow us on Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/burning.boat/ Listen to our Podcast - https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/burning-boat-business/id1395110720

Kadeau Bornholm – Michelin-Starred Beach Cottage on an Island
description: Kadeau is the crown jewel of Bornholm’s dining scene. Chef Nicolai Nørregaard and his childhood friends Rasmus and Magnus Kofoed transformed a humble beachside cottage into a Michelin-starred eatery that showcases ingredients native to the island of Bornholm. Kadeau was one of the first restaurants to embrace the New Nordic manifesto, and took it even further by focusing on the natural products of their small island – many of the vegetables and herbs come from Kadeau’s own garden. The restaurant has been among the top in Scandinavia since it first opened in 2007, but now after overcoming a bankruptcy, surviving a global pandemic, and undergoing a massive renovation, the team is stronger than ever (and the new open kitchen is stunning). title: COOL DH - a pioneering project for district heating solutions
description: In this film we invite you to Brunnshög where the Swedish part of COOL DH is taking place. At Brunnshög, Kraftringen is now building the world's largest grid for low temperature district heating. The objective of COOL DH action is to support cities in their endeavor to plan and deploy new, efficient district heating and cooling (DHC) systems and extend, refurbish existing ones to higher standards. Thus allowing greater uptake of renewables, recovering of excess heat or cold while improving the overall efficiency of the systems. COOL DH is an abbreviation of Cool ways of using low grade Heat Sources from Cooling and Surplus Heat for heating of Energy Efficient Buildings with new Low Temperature District Heating (LTDH) Solutions. The project is funded by Horizon 2020 – the biggest EU Research and Innovation programme implementing the Innovation Union, a Europe 2020 flagship initiative aimed at securing Europe’s global competitiveness. The COOL DH consortium consists of the utilities and municipalities of the two cities Lund (SE) and Høje-Taastrup (DK) and leading DH energy specialists as well as leading industrial manufacturers.

description: Breakfast Day 6 Breakfast: Apple Juice Alpro Blueberry Soya Yogurt with calcium and vanilla Earl Gray Tea with Stevia tablets Water Pukka Brand Licorice & Cinnamon tea, Coco tea with coffee Okologisk Oat Milk by Naturli Brand. Okologisk=Organic in Danish language. Sugar Free Riccola Biotene dry mouth lozenge Tea and Sorbet at Conditoriet La Glace: I thought the window decorations, were so eye-catching and pleasant for the holidays. vegan Raspberry and Apricot Orange Sorbet. La Glace Tea I ordered: Black Tea with Orange, Vanilla, Roses and Cinnamon. I had a refill of tea, by using warm water from the water glass station. I added warm tap water to the tea, to get extra tea. I just couldn't resist La Glace tea. One of my favorites is High Tea, a tradition in England. I had high tea once in British Columbia, Canada. Lunch: Chick-Free by Naturli. "Be the change." It's basically hydrated soy slice chunks. Vegan gluten fri Pate by Zwergenwiese, shitake (streich) Sendi mustard dill vegan gluten fri pate. left over vegan gluten fri rice focaccia Marschland Naturkost Rotkohl Rotkohl-red cabbage Rotkohl ingredients: red cabbage shredded, wasser(german)-water, apfelsaft(german)-applejuice, brown sugar, Meerzals, Nelke www.alfredpaulsen.de. A baking hat, measuring spoons, fox eye mask and gloves, rolling pin, cups about Copenhagen, lip balms from Apotek, Flying Tiger and Irma Grocery Store, 2 unicorn plush hats-white and pink colors. title: Danish food!
VEGAN DANISH SUMMER DESSERT (KOLDSKÅL)
description: "Koldskål" is a traditional Danish summer dessert. Usually with buttermilk and eggs - but you can make it vegan. And it is refreshing and cooling on a warm summer day. Ingredients 1. 750 g Alpro yohurt* with lime/lemon (or vanilla, neutral) or homemade plant-based yohurt 2. 300 g plant milk (oats, soy, rice, almond etc.) 3. 2 tbsp. sugar 4. 1/4 tsp. vanilla powder or some vanilla extract 5. Zest from 1 lemon 6. Juice from ½ lemon Garnish 1. Lemon zest 2. Chopped lemon balm 3. Smal lemon balm leaves 4. Sliced strawberries Add 1. Vegan biscuits You can print the recipe here: (English) http://www.ayuspirit.com/danishsummerdessert/ (Danish) http://www.ayuspirit.dk/koldskaal/ Media: Instagram: @ayuspirit Websites: https://www.ayuspirit.com and https://www.ayuspirit.dk (se og print opskrift) Music: April Fools, Andreas Ericson, http://www.epidemicsound.com/ Old photos: https://www.fotohistorie.com/koslashkkener.html (Odense Bys Museer) http://www.svendborghistorie.dk/historier/erhverv/131-braendeskov-mejeri About the history of koldskål and kammerjunkere: https://videnskab.dk/sporg-videnskaben/hvorfor-hedder-det-kammerjunkere http://madhistorie.dk/opskrifter/andet30.html

title: JEG VAR VEGANER I TRE DAGE - så svært var det... LouLiving
description: 🍕 ÅBEN DENNE SMUKKE BOKS, FOR NYTTIG INFORMATION OG LÆKKER VIRTUEL PIZZA🍕 I dag er det tid til den ultimative klimatosse challenge for mig - jeg skulle nemlig spise VEGANSK i hele tre dage! Køb min bog her: www.louliving.dk OPSKRIFT PÅ PASTA: Ingredienser 300g pasta Salt 300g blandede svampe ½ løg 1 fed hvidløg 1 dl soya cuisine Et par kviste timian Citron Salt Peber TO DO: Sæt en gryde med vand over. Hak løg og hvidløg fint og bland det med timian. Skær svampe i mundrette stykker. Kog pastaen. Steg svampe i lidt olie ved høj varme. Skru ned til mellem varme og tilsæt løg, hvidløg og timian. Dræn pastaen (gem 1 dl ”pastavand”). Tilsæt pasta til svampeblandingen og bland sammen med pastavand. Tag panden af varmen og tilsæt soya cuisine og lad det tykne 1 minut ved omrøring. Smag til med citron, salt, lidt ekstra timian og masser af knust sort HUSK AT subscribe til min second channel LOUTUBEN, der uploader jeg hele tiden wuhuu: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJ1zrSjeKHvsPc710994Kew Se mit merch her!: www.loulivingshop.dk BLIV EN LOUVER I DAG! 💁 FIRMA KONTAKT: contact@louliving.dk 🙅 INSTAGRAM: @louliving 🙆 BLOG: www.louliving.dk 🙋 FACEBOOK GRUPPE: https://www.facebook.com/groups/1029586820480184/
